findings.description: Title and section heading styling differs from the source.
findings.evidence: On page 1, the PDF title is centered with serif styling and underlined section headings, while the HTML uses left-aligned sans-serif text with a horizontal rule and non-underlined headings. Content remains distinct and readable.

findings.description: Section heading formatting is simplified in HTML.
findings.evidence: On page 2, the PDF shows an underlined, centered 'Results' heading; the HTML shows a left-aligned heading without underline. Body text content is preserved.

findings.description: Summary heading formatting differs from the source.
findings.evidence: On page 3, the PDF uses a centered, underlined 'Summary' heading, while the HTML uses a left-aligned heading without underline. The summary text and map placement are preserved.
